testsuite.name: Fazer busca de destinos, selecionar datas, compra de passagens, selecionar assentos e finalizar compra

testcase.name: Fazer busca de destinos, selecionar datas, compra de passagens, selecionar assentos e finalizar compra Fazer busca de destinos IDA com 1 passageiro
testcase.classname: Fazer busca de destinos IDA com 1 passageiro

testcase.name: Fazer busca de destinos, selecionar datas, compra de passagens, selecionar assentos e finalizar compra Fazer busca de destinos IDA com 2 passageiros
testcase.classname: Fazer busca de destinos IDA com 2 passageiros

failure.message: Timed out retrying after 90000ms: Expected to find element: `li[data-js^="offer-element-"]`, but never found it.

testcase.failure: AssertionError: Timed out retrying after 90000ms: Expected to find element: `li[data-js^="offer-element-"]`, but never found it. at Context.eval (webpack://cypress-treino/./cypress/support/commands.js:277:62) at wrapped (https://www.viacaocometa.com.br/__cypress/runner/cypress_runner.js:146279:43)

testcase.name: Fazer busca de destinos, selecionar datas, compra de passagens, selecionar assentos e finalizar compra Fazer busca de destinos IDA e Volta com 2 passageiros
testcase.classname: Fazer busca de destinos IDA e Volta com 2 passageiros

failure.message:
`cy.click()` failed because the page updated as a result of this command, but you tried to continue the command chain. The subject is no longer attached to the DOM, and Cypress cannot requery the page after commands such as `cy.click()`.
Common situations why this happens: - Your JS framework re-rendered asynchronously - Your app code reacted to an event firing and removed the element
You can typically solve this by breaking up a chain. For example, rewrite:
> `cy.get('button').click().should('have.class', 'active')`
to
> `cy.get('button').as('btn').click()` > `cy.get('@btn').should('have.class', 'active')`
https://on.cypress.io/element-has-detached-from-dom

testcase.failure:
CypressError: `cy.click()` failed because the page updated as a result of this command, but you tried to continue the command chain. The subject is no longer attached to the DOM, and Cypress cannot requery the page after commands such as `cy.click()`.
Common situations why this happens: - Your JS framework re-rendered asynchronously - Your app code reacted to an event firing and removed the element
You can typically solve this by breaking up a chain. For example, rewrite:
> `cy.get('button').click().should('have.class', 'active')`
to
> `cy.get('button').as('btn').click()` > `cy.get('@btn').should('have.class', 'active')`
https://on.cypress.io/element-has-detached-from-dom at isAttached (https://www.viacaocometa.com.br/__cypress/runner/cypress_runner.js:153228:58) at validateType (https://www.viacaocometa.com.br/__cypress/runner/cypress_runner.js:153098:9) at Object.isType (https://www.viacaocometa.com.br/__cypress/runner/cypress_runner.js:153133:7) at $Cy.pushSubject (https://www.viacaocometa.com.br/__cypress/runner/cypress_runner.js:146732:22) at wrapped (https://www.viacaocometa.com.br/__cypress/runner/cypress_runner.js:146286:19) at <unknown> (https://www.viacaocometa.com.br/__cypress/runner/cypress_runner.js:152285:15) at tryCatcher (https://www.viacaocometa.com.br/__cypress/runner/cypress_runner.js:1777:23) at Promise._settlePromiseFromHandler (https://www.viacaocometa.com.br/__cypress/runner/cypress_runner.js:1489:31) at Promise._settlePromise (https://www.viacaocometa.com.br/__cypress/runner/cypress_runner.js:1546:18) at Promise._settlePromiseCtx (https://www.viacaocometa.com.br/__cypress/runner/cypress_runner.js:1583:10) at _drainQueueStep (https://www.viacaocometa.com.br/__cypress/runner/cypress_runner.js:2381:12) at _drainQueue (https://www.viacaocometa.com.br/__cypress/runner/cypress_runner.js:2370:9) at Async._drainQueues (https://www.viacaocometa.com.br/__cypress/runner/cypress_runner.js:2386:5) at Async.drainQueues (https://www.viacaocometa.com.br/__cypress/runner/cypress_runner.js:2256:14)
